bottleneck: The current accepted claim is a single strided-point measurement without a matched cache-friendly control, so the locality hypothesis is not yet directly tested within the same benchmark family and launch configuration.
expected_gain: A paired control run would convert the current point observation into a direct within-config comparison, improving interpretability of memory-hierarchy effects while staying inside the existing workflow and budget.
proposal: Run one rollbackable follow-up spec in the same CUDA memory probe handler using identical launch parameters and deterministic controls but a cache-friendly access pattern, then analyze the result as a paired comparison against spec_3626106571cf. Keep all records append-only, link the new spec to the existing claim as a control/baseline comparison target, and preserve the same workflow stages from question selection through plan_next_step.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: When a hypothesis asserts relative performance (for example, 'lower than a cache-friendly regime'), require the next generated spec to include either an explicit matched control_run_target reference or a written reason why no control is needed.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This would reduce unsupported comparative claims and make later human review of benchmark intent and causal interpretation easier.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: Accept comparative claims for curation only if the analyzed run references a matched baseline/control spec under the same fixed launch configuration, or else downgrade the claim to a single-condition observation.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This preserves rigor without changing the append-only history model.

risk: If the chosen control pattern is not truly cache-friendly or not perfectly matched, the comparison may still be ambiguous; an extra run also consumes part of the round budget.
rollback_condition: If the paired control run cannot be executed deterministically within the 120-second round budget or produces incompatible metadata, keep the current single-condition claim as the latest accepted result and do not promote any comparative conclusion.

bottleneck: The workflow now has a matched stride-control pair at one 2048 KB working-set size, but it still cannot test whether the bandwidth gap is primarily a capacity/locality transition versus a stride-only effect because no second working-set point has been measured under the same hostile stride.
expected_gain: A single smaller-working-set rerun at the same 128-byte stride would add an orthogonal comparison axis and make the locality interpretation more defensible without changing the benchmark family, controls, or append-only workflow.
proposal: Run one rollbackable follow-up spec in the existing `cuda_memory_hierarchy_probe` handler with identical launch parameters and deterministic controls, keeping `stride_bytes=128` but reducing `working_set_kb` to a cache-plausible size such as 256 KB. Analyze it only as a paired capacity test against `spec_3626106571cf` and the accepted 4-byte-stride control `spec_ea7294e5fd1a`: if bandwidth rises materially versus the 2048 KB, 128-byte-stride point, record a narrower claim that performance is sensitive to working-set size under hostile stride; if it does not, keep the current conclusion limited to stride-conditioned observations. Preserve all stages and append-only history, and do not promote any cache-level attribution from a single added point.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: When proposing a next spec after a successful matched control, require the agent to name the next comparison axis explicitly (`stride`, `working_set`, or `launch_shape`) and cite the prior spec ids it will hold constant.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This makes later human review of experimental intent easier and reduces under-specified follow-up runs.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: For memory-hierarchy claims, allow cache-level language only after at least one controlled working-set comparison is present; otherwise label results as locality- or capacity-sensitive observations only.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This preserves rigor while keeping curation aligned with the evidence actually collected.

risk: A 256 KB point may still be ambiguous if it does not clearly separate cache-resident from non-resident behavior, so the added run could yield weak evidence without fully resolving the memory-level interpretation.
rollback_condition: If the added 128-byte-stride working-set comparison cannot be executed deterministically within the 120-second budget, produces incompatible metadata, or shows no stable signal on verification, retain the current accepted pair of single-condition claims and do not elevate the locality interpretation beyond the existing matched-stride comparison.

bottleneck: The evidence now covers three cells of a 2x2 comparison (`working_set_kb` in {2048, 256} and `stride_bytes` in {128, 4}), but the 256 KB, 4-byte-stride control is still missing, so the workflow cannot yet distinguish a pure working-set effect from a stride-by-capacity interaction under matched launch parameters.
expected_gain: One bounded follow-up would complete the minimal comparison matrix, making the next analysis materially stronger: reviewers could compare stride effects at both working-set sizes and compare working-set effects at both strides without changing benchmark family, controls, or workflow stages.
proposal: Run one rollbackable follow-up spec in the existing `cuda_memory_hierarchy_probe` handler with the same launch shape and deterministic controls as `spec_3626106571cf`, `spec_ea7294e5fd1a`, and `spec_d89a9f30c9b4`, but set `working_set_kb=256` and `stride_bytes=4`. Analyze it only as the missing matched control for the current 2x2 design: if the 4-byte stride remains substantially faster than 128-byte stride at 256 KB and the 256 KB, 4-byte result is similar to or above the 2048 KB, 4-byte control, record a narrow claim that hostile-stride penalty persists even when the working set is reduced; if not, keep conclusions limited to observed configuration-specific bandwidth differences. Preserve append-only history, keep cache-level attribution out of curated knowledge, and treat this as a matrix-completion step rather than a new benchmark family.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: When a proposal is extending a controlled parameter matrix, require the spec generator to name the matrix axes, list the already-measured cells by spec id, and identify the single missing cell the next run will fill.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This makes experimental coverage explicit for human review and reduces redundant follow-up specs.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: For memory-hierarchy analysis, permit interaction language only after both values of each declared axis have at least one matched comparison; otherwise restrict wording to single-axis or configuration-specific observations.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This preserves rigor and prevents over-interpreting partial factorial evidence.

risk: The added 256 KB, 4-byte result may still be noisy or may show little separation, which would complete the matrix structurally but still leave the mechanistic interpretation weak.
rollback_condition: If the 256 KB, 4-byte-stride run cannot be executed deterministically within the 120-second budget, produces incompatible metadata, or fails independent rerun stability, keep the current accepted claims as-is and do not elevate the analysis beyond the existing partial matrix.

bottleneck: The 2x2 CUDA probe matrix is now structurally complete and each cell has a matching rerun, but the workflow has not yet converted those four configuration-specific claims into one conservative matrix-level analysis, so the system still lacks a reviewed statement about whether the stride penalty persists at both working-set sizes and how large the effect appears.
expected_gain: A single matrix-analysis step can turn four verified point measurements into one higher-value but still narrow conclusion, while preserving append-only history and avoiding unnecessary new benchmark execution before the current evidence is fully exploited.
proposal: Run one rollbackable analysis-only step in `workflow_v1` that consumes the four accepted, verified cells in the completed 2x2 matrix (`working_set_kb` in {2048, 256}, `stride_bytes` in {128, 4}) and produces a matrix-level comparison artifact. Require the analyzer to compute only matched ratios/deltas already supported by accepted evidence: stride effect at 2048 KB, stride effect at 256 KB, working-set effect at 128-byte stride, and working-set effect at 4-byte stride. Permit a narrow claim only if it stays descriptive and tied to this probe family, for example that 4-byte stride measured substantially higher bandwidth than 128-byte stride at both tested working-set sizes under identical launch parameters; keep cache-level or causal attribution out of curated knowledge unless later evidence is added. Preserve append-only history, keep benchmark family and single-GPU scope unchanged, and route any resulting matrix-level claim through the normal verification stage before curation.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: When all declared cells of a controlled matrix are complete, require the analyzer to emit a fixed comparison table listing each matched ratio/delta it computed and the exact claim ids used for each comparison.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This makes matrix-level reasoning auditable for human review and prevents silent cross-run mixing.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: For curated knowledge, allow matrix-level summary claims only when every contributing cell has both an accepted claim and an accepted independent rerun; otherwise keep outputs at per-configuration granularity.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This preserves rigor when elevating from single-cell observations to comparative conclusions.

risk: Because each cell still comes from limited repeat counts and single reruns, a matrix-level analysis may overstate stability if phrased too strongly; the main failure mode is comparative wording that outruns the evidence rather than a workflow invariant breach.
rollback_condition: If the analyzer cannot map all four comparisons to accepted claim ids with matched benchmark metadata, or if human review finds the matrix-level wording causal, cache-specific, or broader than the verified evidence supports, discard the matrix-level artifact and retain only the existing per-cell accepted claims and verifications.

bottleneck: The 1024 KB, 128-byte midpoint is now measured and rerun-verified, but the matched 1024 KB, 4-byte control is still missing, so the workflow cannot yet quantify the stride penalty at the midpoint or compare how that penalty changes across 256, 1024, and 2048 KB.
expected_gain: One additional matched-control benchmark would turn the current partial midpoint result into a complete 3x2 grid, enabling a narrow, auditable comparison of stride sensitivity at all three working-set sizes.
proposal: Run one rollbackable `workflow_v1` benchmark step for `cuda_memory_hierarchy_probe` at `working_set_kb=1024` and `stride_bytes=4`, keeping the existing fixed controls unchanged (`blocks=80`, `threads_per_block=128`, `iterations=20000`, `repeat_count=3`, `seed=7`, `sleep_ms=10`, single GPU, 120-second budget). Treat the output first as a per-configuration claim only; if and only if that claim also receives an accepted independent rerun, allow the analyzer to compute descriptive stride ratios/deltas for the three matched rows `{256,1024,2048} KB` and prepare a reviewable matrix artifact. Keep any resulting language strictly observational and probe-specific, with no cache-level or causal attribution, and preserve the normal append-only history, verification, and human-review gates before curation.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: When proposing a new run to complete a matched-control row or column, require the analyzer to list the exact comparison pairs that the run unlocks and the non-axis metadata keys that must remain identical for each comparison to be valid.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This keeps row-level comparisons auditable and prevents silent mixing of incompatible benchmark configurations.

proposed_profile_updates.instruction_note: Allow row-level comparative claims only when every cell in that row has both an accepted claim and an accepted independent rerun; otherwise keep outputs at single-configuration granularity.
proposed_profile_updates.reason: This preserves rigor when elevating a new midpoint control measurement into stride-effect conclusions.

risk: The main risk is overinterpreting sparse three-point comparisons from limited repeat counts; the workflow remains safe if claims stay descriptive and tied to matched metadata.
rollback_condition: If the 1024 KB, 4-byte run fails, cannot be matched exactly to the existing launch metadata except for `stride_bytes`, or does not receive accepted independent rerun verification, discard the comparative follow-on and retain only the existing accepted per-configuration claims.
